content:
# Ruth Chapter 2 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 880-881 | **Source Image:** RUT_002_page_001.jpg, RUT_002_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Now Naomi had a kinsman of her husband's, a man of great worth, from the family of Elimelech, and his name was Boaz.

**2** And Ruth the Moabitess said to Naomi, "Let me go, I pray, to the field, and let me glean among the ears of grain after him in whose eyes I may find favor." And she said to her, "Go, my daughter."

**3** And she went, and she came and gleaned in the field after the reapers. And it happened that the portion of the field belonged to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.

**4** And behold, Boaz came from Bethlehem and said to the reapers, "The LORD be with you." And they said to him, "The LORD bless you."

**5** And Boaz said to his servant who was set over the reapers, "Whose young woman is this?"

**6** And the servant who was set over the reapers answered and said, "She is a young Moabite woman who returned with Naomi from the fields of Moab.

**7** And she said, 'Let me glean, I pray, and gather among the sheaves after the reapers.' And she came and has remained from the morning until now; she has rested in the shelter but little."

**8** And Boaz said to Ruth, "Have you not heard, my daughter? Do not go to glean in another field, and do not pass on from here, but stay close to my young women.

**9** Let your eyes be on the field which they are reaping, and go after them. Have I not commanded the young men not to touch you? And when you are thirsty, go to the vessels and drink from what the young men have drawn."

**10** And she fell upon her face and bowed down to the ground, and she said to him, "Why have I found favor in your eyes, that you should take notice of me, seeing that I am a foreigner?"

**11** And Boaz answered and said to her, "It has been fully reported to me, all that you have done for your mother-in-law after the death of your husband — that you left your father and your mother and the land of your birth, and came to a people whom you did not know before.

**12** May the LORD repay your work, and may your wages be full from the LORD, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge."

**13** And she said, "May I find favor in your eyes, my lord, for you have comforted me, and you have spoken to the heart of your maidservant, though I am not even as one of your maidservants."

**14** And Boaz said to her at mealtime, "Come here and eat of the bread, and dip your morsel in the vinegar." And she sat beside the reapers, and he held out to her roasted grain. And she ate and was satisfied and had some left over.

**15** And when she rose to glean, Boaz commanded his young men, saying, "Let her glean even among the sheaves, and do not reproach her.

**16** And also pull out some from the bundles for her, and leave them that she may glean, and do not rebuke her."

**17** And she gleaned in the field until evening. And she beat out what she had gleaned, and it was about an ephah of barley.

**18** And she carried it and came into the city, and her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned. And she brought out and gave to her what she had left over after she was satisfied.

**19** And her mother-in-law said to her, "Where did you glean today, and where did you work? May the one who took notice of you be blessed." And she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked, and said, "The name of the man with whom I worked today is Boaz."

**20** And Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, "Blessed be he of the LORD, who has not forsaken His faithful love toward the living and toward the dead." And Naomi said to her, "The man is a near kinsman to us; he is one of our kinsman-redeemers."

**21** And Ruth the Moabitess said, "He also said to me, 'You shall stay close to my young men until they have finished all my harvest.'"

**22** And Naomi said to Ruth, her daughter-in-law, "It is good, my daughter, that you go out with his young women, so that they do not meet you in another field."

**23** And she stayed close to the young women of Boaz to glean until the end of the barley harvest and the wheat harvest. And she dwelt with her mother-in-law.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **אִישׁ גִּבּוֹר חַיִל (ish gibbor chayil), v. 1** — Rendered "a man of great worth." The phrase connotes both material wealth and noble character; it will be echoed in 3:11 when Boaz calls Ruth an אֵשֶׁת חַיִל ("woman of worth/valor").

2. **גֹּאֵל (go'el), v. 20** — Translated "kinsman-redeemer." This legal term denotes a near relative with the right and duty to redeem property and protect the family line. It is the central legal concept of the book and is maintained consistently throughout.

3. **חֶסֶד (hesed), v. 20** — Again "faithful love," now attributed to the LORD. Naomi recognizes God's hesed has not been abandoned — it extends to both the living (Ruth and Naomi) and the dead (Elimelech, Mahlon, Chilion).

4. **כְּנָפָיו (kenafav), v. 12** — "Wings," referring to divine protection. Boaz's blessing that Ruth has come "under the wings" of the God of Israel foreshadows chapter 3, where Ruth will ask Boaz to spread his "wing" (כָּנָף, the same root) over her.

5. **נָכְרִיָּה (nokhriyyah), v. 10** — "Foreigner," a term stronger than גֵּר (resident alien). Ruth identifies herself with the more distant category, making Boaz's kindness all the more remarkable.

6. **מוֹדַעַת / מְיֻדָּע (moda'at), v. 1** — The Leningrad Codex reads מְיֻדַּע with the ketiv/qere distinction. Rendered "kinsman" to capture the sense of a known relative, one who is recognized within the family circle.

— LOGOS
